titleStmt.title: SII 41.78: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 78. (A.R. No. 77 of 1905.) Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District. On a pillar set up within the Muhammandan masjid at Gōripāḷayam (Madurai).

p.hi: Madurai Nayaka, Vīrappa-nāyaka (grand son of Viśvanātha-nāyaka), Śaka 1495, Bhava = 1574 A.D.

summary.p: Records that the king reconfirmed the sale of some six villages made in the past by some Pāṇḍya king to the Mosque called Dilli-oru-kōl-sulutān Paḷḷivāsal at Gōrippāḷayam on the north bank of the Vaigai River in Madurai-vaḷanāḍu. He did this on the authority of some old document. The six villages were Gōrikuḍi, Chokkikuḷam, Allikuḷam, Kaṉṉānēmpal, Chiṟrutūr, and Tiruppālai.
[Royal flag, insignia (suruṭṭi), kettle, bow and arrow are engraved at the top of the inscription.]

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:

ccirāyaṟ pucapelarā

ra kiṣṭṉarāyaravaṟ
kaḷ kāriyattukku kar
ttarāṉa vicuvaṉāta

ppaṉāyakkaṟ vīrappa

ṟkaḷ rāṭciya pari

llā niṉṟa pava

pakaṟaṇamum kūṭi

ḷivācal ko

lli kuḷam

pal ciṟutūṟ

mamum muṉkū
ṉ(ma) pāṇṭiya
rācā 10000 4 1000 ta
ṅkattukku ko
rippāḷaiyattu ni

taṅkattukku vi
laippiramāṇa
m niccaiyittu paṇ

ṟaku rācākkaḷukkum

vantu nām nāyam vi
cārikkumpotu pā
ṇṭiyaṟ korikku
vilaippiramāṇam pa
ṇṇikkuṭutta At
tācciṉai nilavara
māṉapaṭiyiṉāle
muṉ naṭantapaṭikku
nāmum Apimāṉi
ccu korikku viṭ
ṭuk kuṭuttom
Elkai muṉṉam pā
ṇṭiyar ṉāṭṭiṉa
kallu Elkaippaṭi
kku yinta Āru kirā
mattil Uḷḷa ca
kalacamuṣtta Ā
tāyamum cukame
eṇeṇṇai
kkum cūriyappeṟa
vecam cantirappiṟa
vecam Uḷḷa ma
ṭṭukkum cukame Ā
ṇṭu Aṉupavit
tukkoḷḷu
vārākavum §
yiṉimel Ārā
kilum tarkkam paṇ
ṇi torokam
paṇṇiṉāl ke
ṅkaikaraiyilk
kārām pacuve ko
ṇṇa pāvattil
